2603.15960.title: Optimizing Hospital Capacity During Pandemics: A Dual-Component Framework for Strategic Patient Relocation
2603.15960.paper_summary: The COVID-19 pandemic has placed immense strain on hospital systems worldwide, leading to critical capacity challenges. This research proposes a two-part framework to optimize hospital capacity through patient relocation strategies. The first component involves developing a time series prediction model to forecast patient arrival rates. Using historical data on COVID-19 cases and hospitalizations, the model will generate accurate forecasts of future patient volumes. This will enable hospitals to proactively plan resource allocation and patient flow. The second com- ponent is a simulation model that evaluates the impact of different patient relocation strategies. The simulation will account for factors such as bed availability, staff capabilities, transportation logistics, and patient acuity to optimize the placement of patients across networked hospitals. Multiple scenarios will be tested, including inter-hospital trans- fers, use of temporary care facilities, and adaptations to discharge protocols. By combining predictive analytics and simulation modeling, this research aims to provide hospital administrators with a comprehensive decision-support tool. The proposed framework will empower them to anticipate demand, simulate relocation strategies, and imple- ment optimal policies to distribute patients and resources. Ultimately, this work seeks to enhance the resilience of healthcare systems in the face of COVID-19 and future pandemics.
2603.15960.paper_summary_zh: COVID-19 大流行對全球醫院系統造成了巨大的壓力，導致了關鍵的容量挑戰。這項研究提出了一個兩部分的框架，以通過病人轉移策略來優化醫院容量。第一個組件涉及開發一個時間序列預測模型，以預測病人到達率。利用有關 COVID-19 病例和住院的歷史數據，該模型將生成未來病人數量的準確預測。這將使醫院能夠主動規劃資源分配和病人流動。第二個組件是一個模擬模型，用於評估不同病人轉移策略的影響。該模擬將考慮床位可用性、員工能力、運輸後勤和病人病情等因素，以優化病人在網絡醫院之間的安置。將測試多種情景，包括醫院之間的轉移、使用臨時護理設施和對出院協議的調整。通過結合預測分析和模擬建模，這項研究旨在為醫院管理者提供一個全面的決策支持工具。所提出的框架將使他們能夠預測需求、模擬轉移策略並實施最佳政策，以分配病人和資源。最終，這項工作旨在增強醫療系統在面對 COVID-19 和未來大流行時的韌性。
2603.15960.author: Sadaf Tabatabaee et.al.
2603.15960.authors: Sadaf Tabatabaee, Hicham El Baz, Mohammed Khalil Ghali, Nagendra N. Nagarur
